title: poikilogyne lakekamuensis
intro: Poikilogyne lakekamuensis is a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae, native to Papua New Guinea. The plant is believed to possess medicinal properties, with its leaves and stems used to treat various health conditions, including fever and inflammation, due to their anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. Traditional uses of Poikilogyne lakekamuensis include treating respiratory issues, such as coughs and colds, as well as skin conditions like eczema and wounds. Herbal preparations of the plant include infusions, decoctions, and poultices, which are applied topically or consumed as teas to harness its medicinal benefits.
